description: Replace hand-crafted UI elements in side panel app components with the RetroUI primitives (Menu, Badge, Progress), and fix the ConfirmationDialog index tracking bug.

userStories.title: Replace hand-styled dropdown in ConfirmationDialog with retro Menu
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ConfirmationDialog's 'Allow Always' dropdown to use the retro Menu primitive instead of directly styling @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu with raw Tailwind classes.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmationDialog.tsx no longer imports from @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu directly
ConfirmationDialog.tsx imports Menu from the retro Menu component
The AllowAlwaysButton component uses Menu, Menu.Trigger, Menu.Content, and Menu.Item
Visual behavior is identical: dropdown opens above the button, items are clickable, scope selection works
userStories.notes: The AllowAlwaysButton in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx:58-92 currently uses raw @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu with hand-styled classes. Replace with the retro Menu component from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Menu.tsx (created by the previous PRD). The Menu.Content should keep side='top' align='end' sideOffset={4} to match current positioning. Remove the @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu import entirely — the retro Menu wraps it.

userStories.title: Replace inline badge spans in PluginCard with retro Badge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the DEV and SDK badges in PluginCard to use the retro Badge primitive instead of inline-styled spans.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.tsx imports Badge from the retro Badge component
The 'DEV' label (plugin.source === 'local') uses Badge with appropriate variant and size
The 'SDK' label (!plugin.sdkVersion) uses Badge with appropriate variant and size
Both badges render with similar visual weight to the current inline spans (small, subtle)
The SDK badge retains its Tooltip wrapper for the mismatch explanation
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx lines 88-101, there are two inline-styled badge spans. The DEV badge (line 89-91) uses bg-muted text-muted-foreground — Badge variant='default' size='sm' is the closest match. The SDK badge (line 96-98) uses border-accent bg-accent/10 text-accent-foreground — use Badge variant='outline' size='sm' with a className override for accent colors. The badges need to remain visually small (they appear inline with plugin name text) — use size='sm' and add className overrides to shrink text and padding if needed (e.g., text-[9px] px-1 py-0.5 leading-none).

userStories.title: Replace hand-built countdown bar with retro Progress
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ConfirmationDialog's CountdownBar to use the retro Progress primitive instead of hand-building a progress bar with raw divs.
acceptanceCriteria: CountdownBar in ConfirmationDialog.tsx uses Progress from the retro Progress component for the bar
The countdown still shows remaining seconds, updating every COUNTDOWN_POLL_INTERVAL_MS
The progress bar color changes to destructive when fraction drops below 0.33
The overall layout (bar + seconds label in a flex row) is preserved
userStories.notes: The CountdownBar in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx:25-55 hand-builds a progress bar. Replace the outer bg-muted border-border div and inner colored div with the retro Progress component from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Progress.tsx. The @radix-ui/react-progress indicator can be targeted via the Radix data attribute [&>*] or similar CSS child selector in className for the color-change logic (bg-accent-foreground when fraction > 0.33, bg-destructive when <= 0.33). The Progress component accepts a value prop (0-100). Compute the percentage from the fraction. The seconds display span stays outside Progress.

userStories.title: Fix ConfirmationDialog index tracking by ID instead of position
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ConfirmationDialog to track the current confirmation by ID instead of array index so that the viewed item remains stable when confirmations are added or removed.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmationDialog tracks current confirmation by ID (string state) instead of numeric index
When the currently viewed confirmation is removed, the dialog falls back to the first available confirmation
When a new confirmation arrives while viewing an existing one, the current view is not disrupted
Navigation (prev/next) still works correctly with the ID-based approach
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx:95-99, the component uses useState<number>(0) for currentIndex. The bug: if viewing confirmation #3 and confirmations 0-2 are removed, currentIndex=3 is stale. When new confirmations arrive the view jumps unexpectedly. Fix: replace useState<number>(0) with useState<string | null>(null) for currentId. Find the current confirmation by ID in the array. If not found, fall back to confirmations[0]. For prev/next navigation, find the current index from ID, then navigate to confirmations[index-1] or confirmations[index+1] by their ID.
